vocabularies.definition: This is the time in the morning or evening when it is 7:00.
vocabularies.example:
School starts at seven o’clock.
I wake up at seven o’clock.
Dinner is at seven o’clock.

vocabularies.definition: This is the time that is 30 minutes after 12:00.
vocabularies.example:
We have lunch at half past twelve.
It is now half past twelve.
The bus leaves at half past twelve.

vocabularies.definition: This is the time at night when it is 12:00.
vocabularies.example:
The party ended at midnight.
Cinderella left at midnight.
It is dark at midnight.

vocabularies.definition: This is the time in the middle of the day when it is 12:00.
vocabularies.example:
The sun is high at midday.
We eat lunch at midday.
It is hot at midday.

vocabularies.definition: To move your body to music.
vocabularies.example:
They dance to the music.
Can you dance?
We like to dance at parties.

vocabularies.definition: To speak very loudly, often when you are angry or excited.
vocabularies.example:
Don't shout in the library.
He shouted my name.
Please do not shout.

vocabularies.definition: To make a sound with your voice when you think something is funny.
vocabularies.example:
The clowns make us laugh.
She laughed at the joke.
It is good to laugh.

vocabularies.definition: To use your legs to push your body up into the air.
vocabularies.example:
Can you jump high?
The cat jumped onto the wall.
I jump over the puddle.

vocabularies.definition: To jump on one leg.
vocabularies.example:
I could hop on one leg when I was four.
Rabbits hop in the field.
Can you hop on your left foot?

vocabularies.definition: To move with little jumps or to jump over a rope.
vocabularies.example:
She likes to skip in the playground.
I can skip with a rope.
We skip to school.

vocabularies.definition: To wear special clothes, like for a party or a play.
vocabularies.example:
We dress up for the party.
I like to dress up as a pirate.
They dress up in funny clothes.

vocabularies.definition: To go up something using your hands and feet, like a tree or a wall.
vocabularies.example:
Monkeys climb trees very well.
Don't climb that wall.
He climbed the ladder.

vocabularies.definition: To take and hold something that is moving through the air.
vocabularies.example:
Try to catch the ball.
He caught the train.
Can you catch me?

grammars.description: Ôn tập các từ để hỏi: how, what, when, where, which, who, why.
grammars.example:
Which country are we in?
Why are you running?
What time do they have lunch?

grammars.description: Diễn tả khả năng làm gì đó trong quá khứ.
grammars.example:
I could hop on one leg when I was four.
She could read when she was five.
They couldn't swim when they were young.

vocabularies.definition: A hot drink that you make with leaves and water.
vocabularies.example:
Grandma drinks a cup of tea.
Do you like tea?
I want some tea.

vocabularies.definition: A hot, dark drink that many people have in the morning.
vocabularies.example:
Dad likes hot coffee.
This coffee is strong.
She drinks coffee with milk.

vocabularies.definition: A cold drink made with milk and ice cream.
vocabularies.example:
I want a strawberry milkshake.
This chocolate milkshake is good.
Can I have a banana milkshake?

vocabularies.definition: A liquid that you put on food to give it more taste, like tomato or chocolate.
vocabularies.example:
Pass me the tomato sauce.
I like chocolate sauce on ice cream.
The sauce is spicy.

vocabularies.definition: Food made from grain that we usually eat for breakfast with milk.
vocabularies.example:
I have cereal for breakfast.
Do you put milk in your cereal?
This box of cereal is new.

vocabularies.definition: Long, thin pieces of food made from flour, like spaghetti.
vocabularies.example:
We ate a bowl of noodles.
Chicken noodles are yummy.
She cooks noodles for lunch.

vocabularies.definition: A flat, round cake often eaten for breakfast with lemon or sugar.
vocabularies.example:
She made a delicious pancake.
I like lemon on my pancake.
We had pancakes for breakfast.

vocabularies.definition: Small, red, sweet fruits with seeds on the outside.
vocabularies.example:
These strawberries are sweet.
I like strawberries and cream.
Red strawberries are in the garden.

vocabularies.definition: A white, creamy food made from milk.
vocabularies.example:
Yoghurt is good for you.
I eat strawberry yoghurt.
Do you want some yoghurt?

vocabularies.definition: Something you use to drink water or juice, made of a clear material.
vocabularies.example:
A glass of milk.
Please give me a glass of water.
The glass is full.

vocabularies.definition: Something with a handle that you use for drinking tea or coffee.
vocabularies.example:
A cup of tea.
I broke the cup.
Would you like a cup of coffee?

vocabularies.definition: A container for liquid, like water or milk, made of plastic or glass.
vocabularies.example:
A bottle of water.
The baby has a bottle.
Open the bottle please.

vocabularies.definition: A round container used for holding soup, rice, or cereal.
vocabularies.example:
A bowl of soup.
Put the fruit in the bowl.
I ate a bowl of rice.

vocabularies.meaning: đã lái xe (quá khứ của drive)
vocabularies.definition: The past of 'drive' (to move a car or bus).
vocabularies.example:
Yesterday we drove to town.
My dad drove the car.
She drove to the beach.

vocabularies.meaning: đã nhìn thấy (quá khứ của see)
vocabularies.definition: The past of 'see' (to look at something with your eyes).
vocabularies.example:
We saw the place where May grew up.
I saw a bird in the tree.
He saw his friend yesterday.

vocabularies.definition: The past of 'teach' (to help people learn something).
vocabularies.example:
The teacher taught us a song.
My dad taught me to swim.
She taught English last year.

grammars.description: Mệnh đề quan hệ xác định sử dụng who, which, where để làm rõ đối tượng.
grammars.example:
Can you see the woman who is cooking in the kitchen?
This is the town where I lived.
They’re the mountains which I climbed with my father.

grammars.description: Sử dụng thì quá khứ đơn kết hợp với 'when' để kể chuỗi sự việc.
grammars.example:
When we finished lunch we went to the funfair.
When the bus arrived we all got on it.
We were sad when the bus came to take us home.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body between your neck and your arm.
vocabularies.example:
Put your hand on your shoulder.
My shoulder hurts.
She carried the bag on her shoulder.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body that connects your head to your shoulders.
vocabularies.example:
Giraffes have long necks.
My neck is cold.
She wears a scarf around her neck.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body where your food goes after you eat.
vocabularies.example:
My stomach is full.
He has a big stomach.
The food goes into your stomach.

vocabularies.definition: One of the five long parts at the end of your hand.
vocabularies.example:
He pointed his finger at the map.
I have ten fingers.
She cut her finger.

vocabularies.definition: The middle part of your leg that bends.
vocabularies.example:
I hurt my knee when I fell.
Bend your knees.
The baby is on my knee.

vocabularies.definition: One of the five small parts at the end of your foot.
vocabularies.example:
Wiggle your toes.
I stubbed my toe.
He has big toes.

vocabularies.definition: The part in the middle of your arm where it bends.
vocabularies.example:
Don't put your elbows on the table.
My elbow is itchy.
He hurt his elbow playing tennis.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body that is behind you, from your neck to your legs.
vocabularies.example:
He carried the bag on his back.
My back hurts.
Lie on your back.

vocabularies.definition: A long piece of white cloth that you put around a hurt part of your body.
vocabularies.example:
The nurse put a bandage on his arm.
I need a bandage for my cut.
He has a white bandage.

vocabularies.definition: To push air out of your throat with a loud sound when you are sick.
vocabularies.example:
He has a loud cough.
She has a bad cough.
Cover your mouth when you cough.

vocabularies.definition: How hot or cold something is. Doctors check this when you are sick.
vocabularies.example:
She has a high temperature.
The doctor checked my temperature.
I have a temperature today.

vocabularies.definition: When the inside of your neck hurts, often when you speak or eat.
vocabularies.example:
Ice cream is good for a sore throat.
My sore throat hurts when I talk.
He has a cold and a sore throat.

vocabularies.definition: When your back hurts.
vocabularies.example:
Grandpa has backache.
My dad got backache yesterday.
Yoga is good for backache.

vocabularies.definition: When your stomach hurts.
vocabularies.example:
Don't eat too much or you'll get a stomach-ache.
I have a stomach-ache.
She stayed home because of a stomach-ache.

grammars.structure: Review of comparative and superlative adjectives
grammars.description: Ôn tập so sánh hơn và so sánh nhất của tính từ.
grammars.example:
Jenny is taller than you.
Ivan is the strongest man here.
This book is more interesting than that one.

I’m not as strong as you.
The Nile isn't as big as the Amazon River.
Is it as high as Everest?

grammars.description: Cấu trúc muốn hoặc cần làm gì.
grammars.example:
I want to go to the party.
You need to drink water.
She wants to buy a new dress.

vocabularies.definition: The large, round object that shines in the sky at night.
vocabularies.example:
The moon is bright tonight.
There is a full moon.
The moon goes round the earth.

vocabularies.definition: The space above the earth where you can see clouds, the sun, and stars.
vocabularies.example:
There are no clouds in the sky.
The sky is blue.
Birds fly in the sky.

vocabularies.definition: A small, bright light that you see in the sky at night.
vocabularies.example:
Look at that shining star.
There are millions of stars.
The star is very far away.

vocabularies.definition: A place where water falls from a high place down to a lower place.
vocabularies.example:
We swam near the waterfall.
The waterfall is loud.
Water falls down the waterfall.

vocabularies.definition: A hot, wet place with many trees and wild animals.
vocabularies.example:
Many animals live in the jungle.
The jungle is green.
We walked in the jungle.

vocabularies.definition: A living thing that grows in the ground and needs water and sunlight.
vocabularies.example:
Water the plant every day.
This plant has red flowers.
Don't step on the plant.

vocabularies.definition: A line of water that moves across the top of the sea.
vocabularies.example:
The surfer rode the big wave.
The waves are high today.
We jumped over the waves.

vocabularies.definition: The earth and all the people, animals, and places on it.
vocabularies.example:
It's the highest mountain in the world.
We live in a big world.
She wants to travel the world.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of land that has water all around it.
vocabularies.example:
They sailed to a desert island.
We live on an island.
The island has nice beaches.

grammars.description: Trạng từ chỉ cách thức (well, badly, loudly, quietly, quickly, slowly...).
grammars.example:
Please speak quietly.
He runs quickly.
She sings beautifully.

grammars.description: So sánh hơn đối với trạng từ.
grammars.example:
Bears can run more quickly than monkeys.
Monkeys can climb better than tigers.
Elephants can move faster than snakes.

vocabularies.definition: A yellow metal that is very expensive and used for jewelry.
vocabularies.example:
The king wears a gold crown.
She has a gold ring.
The medal is gold.

vocabularies.definition: A shiny, grey metal that is used for jewelry and coins.
vocabularies.example:
She has silver wings.
The car is silver.
I have a silver coin.

vocabularies.definition: Something that has a lot of light or is a very strong color.
vocabularies.example:
He wears a bright orange shirt.
The light is very bright.
She likes bright colours.

vocabularies.definition: A color that is pale and not dark, or something that does not weigh a lot.
vocabularies.example:
Light blue is my favorite color.
This bag is very light.
I need a light jacket.

vocabularies.definition: A color that is close to black, or a place where there is no light.
vocabularies.example:
It's getting dark outside.
He has dark hair.
The room is dark.

vocabularies.definition: A pattern with long lines of different colors.
vocabularies.example:
Zebras are striped animals.
He wore a striped tie.
The flag is striped.

vocabularies.definition: A pattern with many small round dots of different colors.
vocabularies.example:
The clown has spotted trousers.
I saw a spotted dog.
Her dress is spotted.

vocabularies.definition: The part of a bird, insect, or plane that it uses for flying.
vocabularies.example:
Birds have wings to fly.
The fairy has wings.
The plane has big wings.

vocabularies.definition: A thick, stiff kind of paper that we use for making boxes or masks.
vocabularies.example:
This box is made of card.
We use card to make masks.
Draw on the card.

vocabularies.definition: A hard, strong material like iron or gold that we use to make tools or cars.
vocabularies.example:
The scissors are made of metal.
Gold is a metal.
The gate is metal.

vocabularies.definition: A hard material that you can see through, used for windows and bottles.
vocabularies.example:
Be careful, it's made of glass.
The window is glass.
Drink from a glass.

vocabularies.definition: A light material that is easy to shape into things like toys or bottles.
vocabularies.example:
This bottle is made of plastic.
Toys are often plastic.
Don't burn plastic.

vocabularies.definition: The soft hair of a sheep that is used to make warm clothes like sweaters.
vocabularies.example:
My scarf is made of wool.
Sheep give us wool.
This jumper is wool.

vocabularies.definition: The hard material that trees are made of, used to make tables and doors.
vocabularies.example:
The table is made of wood.
We burn wood for fire.
The door is wood.

grammars.description: Hỏi và trả lời về chất liệu của vật.
grammars.example:
What's it made of?
It's made of gold.
They're made of paper.

grammars.structure: shall, could and let's for suggestions
grammars.description: Sử dụng Shall, Could, Let's để đưa ra lời đề nghị.
grammars.example:
Shall we design some props?
We could use blue paper.
Let's design the sea.

vocabularies.definition: The school subject where you learn about maps and different countries.
vocabularies.example:
We learn about maps in geography.
I like geography class.
Geography is interesting.

vocabularies.definition: The school subject where you learn about things that happened in the past.
vocabularies.example:
History tells us about the past.
We study Romans in history.
Do you like history?

vocabularies.definition: Games like football or tennis that you play for exercise.
vocabularies.example:
I play sports on Tuesdays.
Football is a sport.
We do sports outside.

vocabularies.definition: The school subject where you learn about numbers and shapes.
vocabularies.example:
Maths is about numbers.
I am good at maths.
We have a maths test.

vocabularies.definition: The school subject where you learn about nature and how things work.
vocabularies.example:
We do experiments in science.
Science explains the world.
I love science class.

vocabularies.definition: Information Technology - the school subject where you use computers.
vocabularies.example:
We use computers in IT.
IT stands for Information Technology.
We learn coding in IT.

vocabularies.definition: The school subject where you learn to draw and paint.
vocabularies.example:
I love painting in art class.
She is good at art.
We draw in art.

vocabularies.definition: A small computer that you can carry with you.
vocabularies.example:
I've got a new laptop.
Open your laptop.
My laptop is slow.

vocabularies.definition: Programs on a phone or computer that help you do things or play games.
vocabularies.example:
My phone has many apps.
Download this app.
Games are apps too.

vocabularies.definition: A book that you can read on a computer or a tablet.
vocabularies.example:
I read an e-book on my tablet.
Do you like e-books?
This e-book is free.

vocabularies.definition: A way to connect computers and phones to the internet without wires.
vocabularies.example:
Is there wifi in the cafe?
The wifi is fast.
Connect to the wifi.

vocabularies.definition: A bag that you wear on your back to carry things to school.
vocabularies.example:
Put your books in your rucksack.
My rucksack is heavy.
I lost my rucksack.

vocabularies.definition: A book that tells you what words mean.
vocabularies.example:
Look up the word in the dictionary.
Use a dictionary to check spelling.
This is a big dictionary.

vocabularies.definition: A container where you put things that you don't want anymore.
vocabularies.example:
Throw the paper in the bin.
The bin is full.
Where is the bin?

vocabularies.definition: A tool with two blades that you use for cutting paper or cloth.
vocabularies.example:
Use scissors to cut the paper.
The scissors are sharp.
Pass me the scissors.

vocabularies.definition: Something that you use to stick things together.
vocabularies.example:
Stick it with glue.
I need some glue.
The glue is sticky.

grammars.description: Đưa ra lời khuyên nên hoặc không nên làm gì.
grammars.example:
You should listen to your teacher.
You shouldn't talk when your teacher's giving the lesson.
Should you copy in exams? No, you shouldn't.

grammars.description: Diễn tả sự giỏi về một môn học hoặc hoạt động nào đó.
grammars.example:
I'm good at maths.
Are you good at drawing?
He isn't very good at sports.

vocabularies.definition: A person who works in a restaurant and brings food to the table.
vocabularies.example:
The waiter brought our food.
He works as a waiter.
The waiter was polite.

vocabularies.definition: A person who acts in a play or a film.
vocabularies.example:
He wants to be a famous actor.
The actor is in a movie.
She is a good actor.

vocabularies.definition: A person who makes food for people to eat.
vocabularies.example:
The cook makes delicious meals.
My dad is a cook.
The cook is in the kitchen.

vocabularies.definition: A person who drives a car, a bus, or a train.
vocabularies.example:
The bus driver drives carefully.
My uncle is a driver.
Be a safe driver.

vocabularies.definition: A person who writes for a newspaper or a magazine.
vocabularies.example:
A journalist writes news stories.
The journalist interviewed him.
She wants to be a journalist.

vocabularies.definition: A person who takes photos with a camera.
vocabularies.example:
The photographer took a photo.
He is a wildlife photographer.
The photographer has a camera.

vocabularies.definition: A person who paints or draws pictures.
vocabularies.example:
The artist painted a picture.
She is a talented artist.
Picasso was an artist.

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to decide how things will look.
vocabularies.example:
She is a fashion designer.
The designer made this dress.
He wants to be a game designer.

vocabularies.definition: A person who sings songs.
vocabularies.example:
The singer has a beautiful voice.
She is a pop singer.
The singer is on stage.

vocabularies.definition: If something is interesting, you want to know more about it.
vocabularies.example:
This book is very interesting.
The story is interesting.
He is an interesting person.

vocabularies.definition: A kind person is nice and helps other people.
vocabularies.example:
She is kind to animals.
Be kind to your friends.
Thank you, you are very kind.

vocabularies.definition: A friendly person is kind and likes talking to people.
vocabularies.example:
Our new neighbours are friendly.
The dog is friendly.
She gave a friendly smile.

vocabularies.definition: A clever person is good at learning or understanding things.
vocabularies.example:
He is a clever boy.
That is a clever idea.
She is clever at maths.

vocabularies.definition: A lazy person does not like working or being active.
vocabularies.example:
Don't be lazy.
He is too lazy to walk.
The cat is lazy.

vocabularies.definition: A brave person is not afraid of dangerous or difficult things.
vocabularies.example:
Firefighters are very brave.
She was brave at the dentist.
Be brave and try it.

vocabularies.meaning: nhân viên y tế cấp cứu
vocabularies.definition: A person who is trained to help people who are hurt or sick.
vocabularies.example:
The paramedic helped the injured man.
The paramedic drives an ambulance.
Paramedics save many lives.

vocabularies.definition: A person who works in an office and helps with letters and phones.
vocabularies.example:
The secretary answers the phone.
She works as a secretary in an office.
The secretary is typing a letter.

vocabularies.definition: A person who is in charge of a shop, office, or team.
vocabularies.example:
The manager is in a meeting.
Our manager is very kind.
She wants to be a bank manager.

vocabularies.definition: A person who sells and arranges flowers.
vocabularies.example:
The florist made a beautiful bouquet.
I bought these roses from the florist.
The florist works with many flowers.

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to keep or check financial accounts.
vocabularies.example:
The accountant checks the numbers.
My dad is an accountant.
An accountant works with money.

vocabularies.definition: A person who works in a chemist's and gives you medicine.
vocabularies.example:
The pharmacist gave me some medicine.
Ask the pharmacist about this cream.
The pharmacist works in a chemist's.

vocabularies.definition: A person who designs buildings.
vocabularies.example:
The architect designed the school.
An architect draws plans for buildings.
He wants to be a famous architect.

vocabularies.definition: A person who collects rubbish to keep the city clean.
vocabularies.example:
The sanitation worker collects the rubbish.
Sanitation workers keep our city clean.
He is a hardworking sanitation worker.

vocabularies.definition: A person who studies the past by looking at old objects.
vocabularies.example:
The archaeologist found old bones.
An archaeologist studies the past.
They are archaeologists working in Egypt.

grammars.description: Câu điều kiện loại 0 nói về những kết quả luôn đúng.
grammars.example:
When you dance, you look in the mirror.
If you win, you get a big prize.
If William wins, he wants to buy a fantastic new camera.

grammars.description: Phân biệt giữa hỏi về ngoại hình (look like) và tính cách (be like).
grammars.example:
What does your grandad look like?
He's very tall and he's got short, grey hair.
What's your uncle like? He's very friendly.

vocabularies.definition: The side of your body that is not your right side.
vocabularies.example:
Turn left.
The shop is on the left.
Look left and right.

vocabularies.definition: The side of your body that is not your left side.
vocabularies.example:
Turn right.
Raise your right hand.
It is on the right side.

vocabularies.definition: To keep moving in the same direction, not turning left or right.
vocabularies.example:
Go straight on.
Walk straight on for two minutes.
Don't turn, go straight on.

vocabularies.definition: The place where you go to catch a plane.
vocabularies.example:
We're going to the airport.
The plane is at the airport.
The airport is busy.

vocabularies.definition: A place where you can keep your money safe or get more money.
vocabularies.example:
I need to go to the bank.
The bank opens at nine.
He works in a bank.

vocabularies.definition: What you use to buy things, like coins and notes.
vocabularies.example:
He saved his money.
I have no money.
Money is in the bank.

vocabularies.definition: The place where you go to send letters and buy stamps.
vocabularies.example:
Buy stamps at the post office.
Where is the post office?
I mailed a letter at the post office.

vocabularies.definition: A small piece of paper that you stick on a letter before you post it.
vocabularies.example:
Put a stamp on the letter.
I collect stamps.
This stamp is from China.

vocabularies.definition: A shop where you can buy medicine.
vocabularies.example:
We need medicine from the chemist's.
The chemist's is closed.
Go to the chemist's for aspirin.

vocabularies.definition: A place where you go to watch a play or a show.
vocabularies.example:
We watched a play at the theatre.
The theatre is old.
They act in the theatre.

vocabularies.definition: A place where you can see old or interesting objects.
vocabularies.example:
The museum has old objects.
We visited the history museum.
The museum is free.

vocabularies.definition: A place where you pay to stay for a night when you are on holiday.
vocabularies.example:
We stayed in a nice hotel.
The hotel has a pool.
This is a big hotel.

vocabularies.definition: A place where you go to pay for a meal and eat it.
vocabularies.example:
Let's eat at a restaurant.
The restaurant serves pasta.
Is there a restaurant nearby?

grammars.description: Diễn tả dự định trong tương lai hoặc dự đoán.
grammars.example:
I'm going to take my umbrella.
It isn't going to rain.
What are we going to see first?

grammars.description: Giới từ chỉ sự chuyển động: across, into, out of, over, past, round, through.
grammars.example:
We walked through the park.
Go across the bridge.
He went into the house.

vocabularies.definition: Something that is very, very big.
vocabularies.example:
The elephant is huge.
There is a huge building.
The hole is huge.

vocabularies.definition: Something that makes a lot of loud sounds.
vocabularies.example:
The city is very noisy.
Don't be noisy.
The car is noisy.

vocabularies.definition: Something that is very different and you do not see it often.
vocabularies.example:
I saw a strange animal.
It makes a strange sound.
That is a strange hat.

vocabularies.definition: Something that is very, very good.
vocabularies.example:
The food was excellent.
You did an excellent job.
This book is excellent.

vocabularies.definition: Something that is better or more important than other things.
vocabularies.example:
Today is a special day.
I have a special gift.
She is a special friend.

vocabularies.definition: Something that is very bad or nasty.
vocabularies.example:
The weather was horrible.
That smell is horrible.
I had a horrible dream.

vocabularies.definition: A tool that shows you where North, South, East, and West are.
vocabularies.example:
Use a compass to find the way.
The compass points North.
We lost our compass.

vocabularies.definition: A large bag with a handle that you use for carrying clothes when you travel.
vocabularies.example:
Pack your suitcase.
My suitcase is heavy.
Open the suitcase.

vocabularies.definition: A house made of cloth that you sleep in when you go camping.
vocabularies.example:
We slept in a tent.
Pitch the tent here.
The tent is waterproof.

vocabularies.definition: A warm, soft bag that you sleep in when you are in a tent.
vocabularies.example:
My sleeping bag is warm.
Roll up the sleeping bag.
Sleep in the sleeping bag.

vocabularies.definition: A small electric light that you carry in your hand when it is dark.
vocabularies.example:
It's dark, turn on the torch.
I need batteries for the torch.
Shine the torch here.

grammars.description: Mệnh đề thời gian dùng để kể lại trình tự các sự việc trong quá khứ.
grammars.example:
Rose got really wet before I gave her my umbrella.
He ran really fast when Fred came out of the trees.
After we met Grandma's sister in China, we ate the fantastic noodles.

grammars.description: Phân biệt tính từ đuôi -ed (cảm xúc) và -ing (tính chất).
grammars.example:
I was excited because the film was exciting.
This book is boring, I am bored.
She is interested in history, it is interesting.

vocabularies.meaning: thì, là, ở (V2: was/were, V3: been)
vocabularies.definition: To exist or to have a particular quality (V2: was/were, V3: been).
vocabularies.example:
I was at home yesterday.
She has been to London twice.
They were happy together.

vocabularies.definition: To start doing something (V2: began, V3: begun).
vocabularies.example:
The show began at 7 PM.
It has begun to rain.
He began his journey early.

vocabularies.definition: To take something with you to a place (V2: brought, V3: brought).
vocabularies.example:
She brought a cake to the party.
I have brought my books.
He brought his dog along.

vocabularies.definition: To make something by putting parts together, like a house (V2: built, V3: built).
vocabularies.example:
They built a sandcastle.
The house was built in 1990.
He has built a model plane.

vocabularies.definition: To get something by paying money for it (V2: bought, V3: bought).
vocabularies.example:
I bought a new shirt.
She has bought some apples.
We bought tickets for the cinema.

vocabularies.meaning: có thể (V2: could, V3: been able to)
vocabularies.definition: To be able to do something (V2: could).
vocabularies.example:
I could swim when I was five.
She can play the piano.
They couldn't come to the party.

vocabularies.definition: To take and hold something that is moving through the air (V2: caught, V3: caught).
vocabularies.example:
He caught the ball.
The cat has caught a mouse.
I caught a cold yesterday.

vocabularies.definition: To pick one thing from a group of things (V2: chose, V3: chosen).
vocabularies.example:
She chose the red dress.
I have chosen a gift.
He chose to stay home.

vocabularies.definition: To move towards a place or person (V2: came, V3: come).
vocabularies.example:
He came to visit me.
They have come back home.
She came late to class.

vocabularies.definition: To perform an action or a task (V2: did, V3: done).
vocabularies.example:
I did my homework.
She has done a great job.
We did the dishes.

vocabularies.definition: To make a car, bus, or truck move along (V2: drove, V3: driven).
vocabularies.example:
He drove to the beach.
I have never driven a truck.
She drove very carefully.

vocabularies.definition: To put food into your mouth and swallow it (V2: ate, V3: eaten).
vocabularies.example:
I ate a sandwich.
Have you eaten breakfast?
She ate all the cake.

vocabularies.definition: To move down towards the ground suddenly (V2: fell, V3: fallen).
vocabularies.example:
He fell off his bike.
The leaves have fallen.
She fell asleep on the sofa.

vocabularies.definition: To have a particular emotion or physical sensation (V2: felt, V3: felt).
vocabularies.example:
I felt happy yesterday.
She has felt sick all week.
He felt the cold wind.

vocabularies.definition: To see or get something that was lost (V2: found, V3: found).
vocabularies.example:
I found my keys.
She has found a new job.
We found a coin on the street.

vocabularies.definition: To move through the air like a bird or a plane (V2: flew, V3: flown).
vocabularies.example:
The bird flew away.
We have flown to Paris.
The pilot flew the plane.

vocabularies.definition: To not remember something (V2: forgot, V3: forgotten).
vocabularies.example:
I forgot his name.
She has forgotten her keys.
Don't forget to call me.

vocabularies.definition: To receive or obtain something (V2: got, V3: got).
vocabularies.example:
I got a present.
She has got a new car.
We got up early.

vocabularies.definition: To let someone have something as a gift or to use (V2: gave, V3: given).
vocabularies.example:
She gave me a book.
I have given him the money.
They gave us some food.

vocabularies.definition: To move from one place to another (V2: went, V3: gone).
vocabularies.example:
He went to school.
She has gone to the shop.
We went for a walk.

vocabularies.definition: To become bigger or taller (V2: grew, V3: grown).
vocabularies.example:
The plant grew quickly.
He has grown very tall.
We grew vegetables in the garden.

vocabularies.definition: To own something or to eat a meal (V2: had, V3: had).
vocabularies.example:
I had a bike.
She has had lunch.
We had a good time.

vocabularies.definition: To receive sounds through your ears (V2: heard, V3: heard).
vocabularies.example:
I heard a noise.
Have you heard the news?
She heard the phone ring.

vocabularies.definition: To put something where it cannot be seen (V2: hid, V3: hidden).
vocabularies.example:
He hid behind the tree.
The treasure was hidden.
She hid the gift.

vocabularies.definition: To have something in your hand or arms (V2: held, V3: held).
vocabularies.example:
She held the baby.
He held my hand.
I held the door open.

vocabularies.definition: To continue to have something and not give it away.
vocabularies.example:
He kept his promise.
She kept the secret.
We kept walking.

vocabularies.definition: To get new knowledge or skills.
vocabularies.example:
I learnt English.
She has learnt to swim.
We learnt a new song.

vocabularies.definition: To allow someone to do something.
vocabularies.example:
She let me go.
Let him finish.
They let us play outside.

vocabularies.definition: To not be able to find something or to not win a game.
vocabularies.example:
I lost my pen.
He has lost the game.
Don't lose your ticket.

vocabularies.definition: To create or produce something.
vocabularies.example:
She made a cake.
It is made of wood.
I made a mistake.

vocabularies.meaning: có nghĩa là (V2: meant, V3: meant)
vocabularies.definition: To have a particular meaning or sense.
vocabularies.example:
What did you mean?
It meant a lot to me.
I meant to call you.

vocabularies.definition: To see and talk to someone for the first time.
vocabularies.example:
I met him yesterday.
We have met before.
She met her friends at the cafe.

vocabularies.definition: To have to do something because it is very important.
vocabularies.example:
I must go now.
You must listen.
He must be tired.

vocabularies.definition: To move something into a particular place or position.
vocabularies.example:
He put the book on the table.
She put on her coat.
I have put it away.

vocabularies.meaning: cưỡi/lái xe 2 bánh (V2: rode, V3: ridden)
vocabularies.definition: To sit on and control a bike, horse, or motorcycle.
vocabularies.example:
He rode his bike.
I have ridden a horse.
She rode to school.

vocabularies.definition: To move very quickly using your legs.
vocabularies.example:
He ran fast.
She has run a marathon.
We ran for the bus.

vocabularies.definition: To speak words to someone.
vocabularies.example:
He said hello.
She said she was tired.
They said goodbye.

vocabularies.definition: To look at something with your eyes.
vocabularies.example:
I saw a movie.
Have you seen my glasses?
We saw him yesterday.

vocabularies.definition: To make something go to another place, like a letter or email.
vocabularies.example:
I sent a letter.
She has sent an email.
He sent me a gift.

vocabularies.definition: To be on a chair or the floor using your bottom.
vocabularies.example:
He sat on the chair.
We sat down to eat.
She sat next to me.

vocabularies.definition: To use your voice to say words.
vocabularies.example:
He spoke quietly.
I have spoken to him.
She spoke English well.

vocabularies.definition: To be on your feet in an upright position.
vocabularies.example:
He stood up.
She stood by the door.
We stood in line.

vocabularies.definition: To move through water using your arms and legs.
vocabularies.example:
I swam in the pool.
She has swum across the river.
They swam all day.

vocabularies.definition: To get hold of something and move it to another place.
vocabularies.example:
He took a photo.
I have taken the medicine.
She took the bus.

vocabularies.definition: To help someone learn something new.
vocabularies.example:
He taught math.
She has taught me well.
Who taught you that?

vocabularies.definition: To give information to someone by speaking or writing.
vocabularies.example:
He told a joke.
I have told you already.
She told the truth.

vocabularies.definition: To use your mind to have ideas or opinions.
vocabularies.example:
I thought about it.
She thought he was nice.
We thought it would rain.

vocabularies.definition: To make something move through the air from your hand.
vocabularies.example:
He threw the ball.
She has thrown it away.
I threw a stone.

vocabularies.definition: To stop sleeping and become active.
vocabularies.example:
I woke up early.
She has woken up.
The noise woke him.

vocabularies.definition: To have clothes, shoes, or glasses on your body.
vocabularies.example:
She wore a red dress.
I have worn this shirt before.
He wore glasses.

vocabularies.definition: To make words on paper or a screen using a pen or keyboard.
vocabularies.example:
He wrote a book.
I have written a letter.
She wrote her name.

vocabularies.meaning:
một cách cẩn thận
(Type: Adverb)
Noun: care
Adj: careful
vocabularies.definition: To do something in a slow and safe way to avoid mistakes or danger.
vocabularies.example:
Please listen carefully to the instructions.
You must cross the road carefully.
She painted the picture carefully.

vocabularies.meaning:
ồn ào
(Type: Adjective)
Noun: noise
Adv: noisily
vocabularies.definition: Something that makes a lot of loud and unpleasant sounds.
vocabularies.example:
The city is very noisy.
Don't be noisy in class.
The children played noisily outside.

vocabularies.meaning:
dũng cảm
(Type: Adjective)
Noun: bravery
Adv: bravely
vocabularies.definition: Someone who is not afraid of dangerous or difficult things.
vocabularies.example:
The firefighter was very brave.
She bravely fought the dragon.
He showed great bravery.

vocabularies.meaning:
nhanh chóng
(Type: Adverb)
Noun: quickness
Adj: quick
vocabularies.definition: To do something in a very short time or at a high speed.
vocabularies.example:
Run quickly! There's a bear!
She finished her homework quickly.
He gave a quick answer.

vocabularies.meaning:
thân thiện
(Type: Adjective)
Noun: friend
Noun: friendliness
vocabularies.definition: Someone who is kind and pleasant to other people.
vocabularies.example:
Our new neighbours are friendly.
She gave me a friendly smile.
He behaved in a friendly way.

vocabularies.meaning:
lười biếng
(Type: Adjective)
Noun: laziness
Adv: lazily
vocabularies.definition: Someone who does not want to work or be active.
vocabularies.example:
Don't be lazy, help me clean!
The cat stretched lazily in the sun.
His laziness is a problem.

vocabularies.meaning:
xuất sắc
(Type: Adjective)
Noun: excellence
Adv: excellently
vocabularies.definition: Something that is very, very good.
vocabularies.example:
The food was excellent.
She played the piano excellently.
They achieved excellence in their work.

vocabularies.meaning:
hào hứng (cảm xúc)
(Type: Adjective)
Noun: excitement
Adj: exciting (thú vị)
Adv: excitedly
vocabularies.definition: To feel very happy and enthusiastic about something.
vocabularies.example:
I am excited about the trip.
'We won!' he shouted excitedly.
The excitement of the game was huge.

vocabularies.meaning:
một cách nguy hiểm
(Type: Adverb)
Noun: danger
Adj: dangerous
vocabularies.definition: To do something in a way that could cause harm or an accident.
vocabularies.example:
He drove dangerously fast.
Lions are dangerous animals.
Keep out of danger.

vocabularies.meaning:
tốt bụng
(Type: Adjective)
Noun: kindness
Adv: kindly
vocabularies.definition: Someone who is nice and helps other people.
vocabularies.example:
She is kind to animals.
Thank you for your kindness.
He kindly helped me carry the bags.

vocabularies.meaning:
to tiếng, ồn ào
(Type: Adverb)
Noun: loudness
Adj: loud
vocabularies.definition: To make a lot of sound that is easy to hear.
vocabularies.example:
Don't speak loudly in the library.
The music is too loud.
He laughed loudly.

vocabularies.meaning:
kỳ lạ
(Type: Adjective)
Noun: stranger
Adv: strangely
vocabularies.definition: Something that is very different or unusual.
vocabularies.example:
I saw a strange animal.
Strangely, no one was there.
Don't talk to strangers.
